city_personality.slang_reaction: Soon come, but this governance is pure foolishness, man.

opening_sequence.title: Welcome to the Offshore Empire, Governor
opening_sequence.intro: Welcome to George Town. You are managing a financial black hole where shell companies outnumber human beings, dirty money flows beneath pristine coral reefs, and international regulatory bodies are breathing down your neck.

advisors.name: Julian Foster
advisors.hidden_agenda: Protecting a multi-billion dollar private equity fund from international disclosure laws.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Implementing a public registry of beneficial ownership for shell companies.

briefing: Capital is a timid creature, Governor. If you startle it with taxes, it flies to Bermuda.
We have three new shell companies registered to a single mailbox today. Excellent progress.
The European Union is auditing our banking confidentiality protocols again.
agreement: Superb choice. The financial markets love absolute discretion.
Fiscally sound. Our clients will be highly relieved.
disagreement: You are poking the international banking beast. Unwise.
That regulation will cause a capital flight before the markets close.
dialogue.betrayal: You opened our books to the public. My clients' capital has already left, and so have I.
roast: Your understanding of macroeconomics could fit inside a single offshore trust.
I've seen shell companies with more substance than your economic agenda.
scandal_reaction: We must transfer the narrative to a subsidiary account immediately.
This is a severe threat to our jurisdictional reputation.

advisors.name: Inspector Dale Ebanks
advisors.hidden_agenda: Securing exclusive maritime patrol rights to shelter illegal yacht refueling networks.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Diverting maritime security budgets to local community tourism infrastructure.

briefing: Unidentified vessels are lingering just outside territorial waters.
The cruise ship terminal is completely overwhelmed by arrivals.
Customs seized another shipment of undeclared luxury assets at the airport.
agreement: Order maintained. The borders remain secure.
A solid display of structural control, Governor.
disagreement: You are exposing our coastline to unrestricted international elements.
This leaves our ports vulnerable to external interference.
dialogue.betrayal: You stripped our maritime security. Don't look for my cutters when the smugglers arrive.
roast: You couldn't secure a dinghy in a hotel swimming pool.
Our maritime defenses are as shallow as Seven Mile Beach under your command.
scandal_reaction: Lock down the communication feeds. No press leaks.
This requires a full operational media blackout.

advisors.name: Chloe McLaughlin
advisors.hidden_agenda: Enforcing foreign regulatory standards to bankrupt local real estate developers.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Defying an OECD compliance mandate regarding real estate anti-money laundering controls.

briefing: The Financial Action Task Force is reviewing our compliance gray-list status.
Foreign developers are trying to buy up the remaining mangrove properties.
International monitors are demanding we track all real estate wire transfers.
agreement: Excellent. The international community will look favorably upon this transparency.
Compliance achieved. The global banks will keep our pipelines open.
disagreement: This defiance will place us directly onto the European blacklist.
You are acting as if we are an economic superpower. We are a sandbar.
dialogue.betrayal: You refused global compliance. The sanctions are landing tomorrow, and I am signing with Paris.
roast: You are trying to fight the G7 with a tourism budget. Brilliant.
Your policy decisions look like they were drafted by a tax evader on a cocktail napkin.
scandal_reaction: The international press is going to dismantle us for this.
This requires immediate international public relations spin.

advisors.name: Captain Billy Bodden
advisors.hidden_agenda: Banning mega-cruise ships to safeguard local artisanal fishing waters.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Approving a mega-cruise ship berthing facility in George Town harbor.

briefing: The cruise ship tenders are choking the harbor traffic, Governor.
Local fishermen can't even get out past the mega-yachts anymore.
West Bay Road is backed up with tourist rental cars again.
agreement: True Caymanian spirit. The locals will toast to this tonight.
The waters are safe for another day, Governor.
disagreement: You're selling our waters out to foreign cruise lines.
That policy is going to run our local heritage straight aground.
dialogue.betrayal: You destroyed the reef for a corporate dock. The local captains are shutting down the harbor.
roast: You run this infrastructure like you've never left dry land.
This island is sinking under concrete and you're handing out building permits.
scandal_reaction: The boys down at the docks are laughing at your press conference.
Can't hide dirty laundry in water this clear, Governor.

advisors.name: Pastor Thomas Hydes
advisors.hidden_agenda: Banning luxury casino cruise gambling within Caymanian territorial limits.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Granting an offshore gaming license to a high-roller floating casino enterprise.

briefing: The moral fiber of this island is being eroded by foreign excess.
The congregation is worried about the secularization of our local districts.
We must protect our traditional Sunday heritage from corporate greed.
agreement: A righteous path. We must protect our island's soul.
The community elders will support this moral stance.
disagreement: You are inviting absolute decadence into our community.
This decision favors corporate gold over foundational virtue.
dialogue.betrayal: You brought organized gambling into our holy waters. I will rally every pulpit against you.
roast: Your moral integrity is shorter than the winter tourism season.
You worship the almighty dollar more than any banker in George Town.
scandal_reaction: The truth always surfaces in the light of righteousness.
You must repent before the community discovers the rest.

decisions.title: Public Registry Mandate
decisions.body: The UK and EU are demanding the Cayman Islands implement a fully public registry of beneficial ownership for shell companies. Failing to do so risks a crippling financial blacklist, but corporate law firms threaten to leave the jurisdiction if secrecy is compromised.

options.label: Defy the mandate and keep the registries completely confidential.

consequences.public_reaction: Our financial sector remains secure, even if Europe is furious.

options.label: Implement the fully open public registry to appease international watchdogs.

consequences.public_reaction: The lawyers are packing their bags for Switzerland. There go our jobs.

options.label: Create a 'pay-to-view' dark registry where foreign governments must pay millions to see corporate details.

consequences.public_reaction: Extorting the G7 to look at our shell companies? Bold choice, Governor.

decisions.title: George Town Berthing Facility
decisions.body: International cruise lines are offering to fund a massive deepwater berthing dock in George Town. It promises a massive influx of daily tourist spending, but environmental groups warn the dredging will permanently destroy the local coral reefs.

options.label: Reject the dock project entirely to protect the maritime environment.

consequences.public_reaction: The reefs are safe, but the downtown shop owners are weeping.

options.label: Approve the dock and fast-track the coral dredging process.

consequences.public_reaction: Say goodbye to the dive industry, here come ten thousand cruise tourists a day.

options.label: Dredge the harbor but sell the destroyed coral chunks to wealthy expats as luxury souvenirs.

consequences.public_reaction: You are literally commercializing environmental destruction. Horrific.

decisions.title: Expat Work Permit Levy
decisions.body: Local Caymanians are being priced out of real estate due to high-earning financial sector expats. Populists demand an aggressive new surcharge on foreign work permits to subsidize local housing initiatives.

options.label: Maintain the current standard work permit fees to keep corporate stability.

consequences.public_reaction: The expats keep laughing all the way to the beach while locals can't pay rent.

options.label: Impose a massive 30% surcharge on all high-earning foreign work permits.

consequences.public_reaction: Finally, the financial sector pays their share to the people who actually live here!

options.label: Abolish the permit fee entirely but force all expats to sponsor a local family's groceries.

consequences.public_reaction: Corporate feudalism at its absolute weirdest. My accountant buys my plantains now.

decisions.title: Floating Casino Jurisdiction
decisions.body: A luxury cruise syndicate wants to anchor a massive permanent floating casino just outside the barrier reef, requesting a special offshore municipal gaming permit. They promise huge luxury tax revenues, but the local church unions are furious.

options.label: Reject the casino permit on moral and community grounds.

consequences.public_reaction: The churches are happy, but we missed out on millions in corporate cash.

options.label: Approve the floating casino license to boost high-roller tourism.

consequences.public_reaction: Sin on the water. Everyone is taking water taxis out to the blackjack tables.

options.label: Approve it, but mandate that losing bets are automatically converted into local infrastructure bonds.

consequences.public_reaction: Losing my savings on roulette is technically civic duty now. Amazing.

decisions.title: The Seven Mile Ritz Expansion
decisions.body: An international hotel conglomerate wants to clear a vital mangrove wetland reserve to build an ultra-exclusive private villa estate. They are offering an upfront payment to the municipal budget.

options.label: Deny the development permit to preserve the natural hurricane barrier.

consequences.public_reaction: The coast is protected, but the mega-rich will have to look elsewhere to sleep.

options.label: Approve the expansion to secure the foreign capital injection.

consequences.public_reaction: Paving over our environmental buffer for billionaires. Classic Cayman move.

options.label: Allow them to build the resort but only if it is constructed entirely out of confiscated asset materials.

consequences.public_reaction: The luxury villas are literally built out of seized smuggler yachts. Peak aesthetic.

crises.name: EU Blacklist Enforcement
crises.trigger: The European Union has officially placed the Cayman Islands on its non-cooperative tax blacklist.
crises.description: Major global banks are freezing processing lines for assets managed within our borders. Over $300M in transaction capital is stalled in limbo. The international financial lobby demands immediate regulatory changes, while the local industry screams that capitulation is suicide.

options.label: Implement all requested European corporate tax harmonization metrics instantly.
Launch an aggressive international marketing and lobbying counter-campaign to bypass EU clearing houses.
Pivot operations completely to unrestricted dark asset networks in unrecognized jurisdictions.

crises.name: Category 5 Storm Threat
crises.trigger: A massive Category 5 storm has locked onto Grand Cayman with direct impact expected in 48 hours.
crises.description: Evacuation off the island is impossible for 90% of the civilian populace. The low-lying corporate districts face catastrophic storm surges. Emergency storm defenses are underfunded, and local shelters are completely inadequate.

options.label: Enforce mandatory inland evacuation into public government structures.
Requisition all luxury private expat concrete compounds as public emergency storm shelters.
Offer temporary regulatory immunity to any foreign vessel that anchors to act as a breakwater wall.

crises.title: Corporate Capital Flight
crises.trigger: A sudden coordinated regulatory raid on a local law office triggers a mass corporate exodus panic.
crises.description: Over five thousand registered businesses have dissolved their Cayman mailboxes in the last seventy-two hours, shifting registrations to Mauritius. The licensing revenue base is cratering rapidly, threatening a total collapse of the civil service payroll.

options.label: Slash corporate maintenance licensing fees across the entire registry to stabilize numbers.
Seize the remaining local asset balances of any corporation attempting to dissolve without warning.
Automatically merge all dissolving companies into a single giant state-owned mega-monopoly entity.

crises.name: London Constitutional Takeover
crises.trigger: Following continuous reports of financial volatility, the United Kingdom threatens to dissolve local self-governance.
crises.description: The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office has dispatched a Royal Overseer vessel to George Town. They intend to invoke direct colonial rule, strip the governor of local controls, and absorb all asset portfolios directly into the British Treasury structure.

options.label: Relinquish executive governance powers to the Royal Overseer.
Mobilize local constitutional mechanisms and declare full economic sovereignty.
Leak the private offshore financial files of the entire British Cabinet to the global media.

scandals.title: Governor's Personal Shell Account Exposed
scandals.description: Leaked registration data reveals you hold an undeclared beneficial interest in a trust receiving funds from shipping companies.

scandals.title: Seized Asset Yacht Crashed into Marine Reserve
scandals.description: A luxury yacht confiscated by your border patrol was spotted grounded on a coral reef during a private cabinet party.

scandals.title: Governor Manually Approved Cartel Transaction Pipeline
scandals.description: Leaked encrypted logs reveal you personally signed a waiver allowing a sanctioned South American enterprise to clear transactions.

positive: Good job protecting the offshore industry, man.
Finally a Governor who understands capital retention.
The global banks are happy, so my job is secure.
Pragmatic choice. Let Bermuda deal with the red tape.
At least our financial confidentiality remains intact.
Soon come prosperity. Good move, Governor.
neutral: More regulatory paperwork to hide my small savings.
Let's see if this satisfies the regulators in Brussels.
As long as the tourist dollars keep flowing on Seven Mile.
Another compliance amendment. The law firms will get richer.
Just another day in the tax haven.
We'll see if the local housing market changes at all.
negative: The locals get nothing while the billionaires dance.
This administration is selling our entire island out.
I can't even afford to rent a basic room in George Town.
Pure corruption hidden behind corporate legal jargon.
Our reef is being destroyed and the government is applauding.
Wha gwan with these corporate handouts? Ridiculous.
media: Cayman Compass: Parliament debates historic offshore regulatory overhaul.
Cayman News Service: Financial sector warns of severe blacklisting risks.
The Star: Local districts report record high living costs.
Compass Business: Corporate registrations hit new levels under Governor.
Radio Cayman: Callers vent fury over local infrastructure neglect.
International Finance Review: Cayman Islands adjusts secrecy protocols.
politician: The opposition demands immediate disclosure of corporate registry changes.
We must defend our jurisdictional economic sovereignty from external actors.
This administration is actively destroying local Caymanian heritage.
A necessary transition to maintain our competitive global advantage.
We urge the Governor to resist foreign regulatory extortion.
This policy leaves our domestic labor market completely exposed.
activist: Save our mangroves! Stop corporate real estate greenwashing!
Transparency now! End the era of anonymous dirty wealth accounts!
The reefs won't survive another mega-cruise development!
Housing access for Caymanians, not just luxury villas for expats!
Our natural barrier protects us from storms, stop clearing it!
Accountability in public governance is long overdue on this island.
street: Bro, traffic on West Bay Road is absolute madness today.
Can't even buy local fish without corporate pricing anymore.
The lawyers are buying everything up, man.
Another mailbox company registered down the street, who cares?
Just trying to get through the storm season without going broke.
The island is small, but the corporate greed is massive.
crisis: The banks are freezing operations! Absolute panic downtown!
The storm surge is coming over the coastal walls right now!
We are on the EU blacklist, the local economy is paralyzed!
Get inland immediately, the shelters are already packed out!
The corporate capital is fleeing to Mauritius by the millions!
Lord look after the island, total structural failure imminent.
scandal: Caught with your hands directly in the offshore jar! Resign!
They are using seized assets as private luxury party toys.
The corruption here is as transparent as the water.
Absolutely shameless behavior from our public leaders.
They are selling us out to foreign cartels for a quick cut.
Time for London to step in and clean out City Hall completely.

scandal_reactions.minor: Just another politician with a hidden offshore entity, standard business.
scandal_reactions.moderate: They are destroying our natural environment while partying on yachts.
scandal_reactions.major: This completely undermines our global compliance reputation. We face absolute ruin.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: The empire is collapsing. Pack your bags before the federal marshals land.

romance_exposure.flavour: The corporate lawyers don't care, but the local church congregations are reading out your text messages from the pulpit.
